info.title: SEC API
info.description: SEC API for investor agents spanning SEC filings, market, macro, factor, portfolio, and intelligence-query workloads.

get.summary: Return OAuth protected resource metadata for hosted MCP and API clients
Return or proxy OAuth authorization server metadata for the configured WorkOS tenant
Return the current authenticated principal and organization context
Return the current organization profile
List operator-visible organizations with support-safe plan, usage, and recent activity summaries
Return an operator-facing organization snapshot with API keys, billing, usage, events, webhooks, streams, and delivery state
Return operator-scoped request diagnostics for a tenant request identifier
Return operator-scoped delivery aggregates for webhook and stream activity within a tenant

post.summary: Run operator-scoped hot-lane polling to enqueue fresh SEC publication observations
Run operator-scoped reconcile work to enqueue missing live hot-lane filings
Run operator-scoped ingest worker passes to materialize queued filing artifacts and sections
Export an operator-scoped ingest queue and checkpoint summary from the running SEC API service
Warm search-visible manifests for the latest market-wide SEC filings by core form
Warm operator-scoped market snapshots, reference, estimates, bars, and corporate-action caches for covered symbols
Bootstrap operator-scoped Tier-1 macro materialization for launch countries
Warm operator-scoped factor returns, exposures, and correlations for covered symbols and factor categories
Warm operator-scoped intraday factor snapshots for supported categories and windows
Backfill FIGI-family and security identifier coverage into the canonical entity store from operator-supplied universe rows
Receive and process signed Stripe subscription lifecycle webhooks

get.summary: Return the current organization's billing snapshot, including pricing posture, budget controls, and settlement provider state
Return the public pricing catalog, plan metadata, and meter-family launch rates

post.summary: Create a Stripe Checkout setup session that activates Pay As You Go card-on-file billing
Quote a billable workflow or meter class against the current billing plan and budget gates

put.summary: Update organization-level spend caps, soft caps, and approval thresholds for PAYG usage

post.summary: Create a Stripe Checkout session for a self-serve Personal or Team SEC API plan
Create a Stripe Billing Portal session for the current organization

get.summary: List API keys for the current organization

post.summary: Create a new API key for the current organization
Issue a short-lived, single-use sponsor token for agent bootstrap under the current organization
Exchange a sponsor token for the first org-scoped API key, billing snapshot, limits, and MCP install metadata

get.summary: Return the logged-in dashboard overview with principal, org, enriched billing, usage, and API key context
Return profile, organization, appearance, security, and account-deletion settings for the dashboard

patch.summary: Update local dashboard profile preferences for the current WorkOS user
Update locally owned organization profile settings

put.summary: Update dashboard appearance preferences for the current organization

post.summary: Record an account-deletion request for the current organization without deleting data inline

get.summary: Return dashboard usage counts over time for the current organization
Return dashboard endpoint-level usage breakdown for the current organization
Return recent dashboard request log rows for the current organization
Export dashboard usage request log rows as JSON or CSV

200.description: Dashboard usage export as JSON or CSV

get.summary: Return dashboard activity totals and recent request rows for the current organization
List canonical event, webhook delivery, and stream records for the current organization
Export filtered canonical events as JSON or NDJSON for support and operations
Return request-id scoped diagnostics across usage, events, deliveries, streams, and artifacts
Return aggregated webhook and stream delivery summaries for support workflows
Return operator-only observability configuration and provider seam status
Return an operator-only observability export with config, usage, and recent events
List webhook endpoints for the current organization

post.summary: Create a signed webhook endpoint for artifact and stream events
Rotate the signing secret for a webhook endpoint

get.summary: List canonical delivery attempts for a webhook endpoint

post.summary: Replay a stored webhook delivery attempt by re-emitting its source event to the endpoint

get.summary: List saved-search monitors for the current organization

post.summary: Create a saved-search monitor with optional webhook or email delivery destination

get.summary: Retrieve a single monitor by id
Run the monitor's saved query and return new matches since last_checked_at

post.summary: Update or replace the monitor's delivery destination (e.g., set or change an email recipient)

get.summary: Render an unsubscribe confirmation page for a signed token
get.description: Anonymous endpoint. GET is preview-safe and never mutates state — it only renders a confirmation page with a POST form. Email link previewers (Outlook, Apple Mail, Gmail) will fetch this URL during scanning; the user must submit POST to actually unsubscribe (RFC 8058).

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Signed unsubscribe token (HMAC-SHA256, with nonce + expiry).

post.summary: Unsubscribe from monitor email delivery (RFC 8058 one-click compatible)
post.description: Anonymous endpoint. Token may be supplied in form-encoded body OR query string. When the body includes `List-Unsubscribe=One-Click`, the response is 204 No Content per RFC 8058. Idempotent — repeated calls with the same token return the same result.

get.summary: List stream subscriptions for the current organization

post.summary: Create a stream subscription for event polling and replay

get.summary: Poll canonical stream events for a subscription with cursor semantics

post.summary: Mint a short-lived signed WebSocket stream ticket for the current principal
post.description: Creates a short-lived signed ticket that can be sent as the `ticket` query parameter when upgrading to `/v1/stream/ws`. This avoids sending long-lived API keys or bearer tokens in browser-visible WebSocket URLs.

get.summary: Upgrade to a WebSocket connection for real-time filing event streaming
get.description: Upgrades the HTTP connection to a WebSocket. Clients must supply a short-lived signed `ticket` query parameter minted from `POST /v1/stream/tickets`. Streams `filing.published` events in real time with optional form/ticker filtering and cursor-based replay. Metered as `stream_connection` on upgrade and `stream_event` per delivery (1000/min default).

parameters.name: ticket
parameters.description: Short-lived signed stream ticket minted from `POST /v1/stream/tickets`.

parameters.name: forms
parameters.description: Comma-separated form types to filter (e.g. '10-K,8-K'). Case-insensitive.

parameters.name: tickers
parameters.description: Comma-separated tickers to filter (e.g. 'AAPL,MSFT'). Case-insensitive.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Resume from a previous event cursor (e.g. 'sevt_...'). Events after this cursor are replayed on connect.

101.description: WebSocket upgrade successful. Server sends JSON frames: connected, filing.published, pong, filters_updated, rate_limited.

get.summary: Resolve an entity by ticker, CIK, FIGI-family identifier, ISIN, CUSIP, or name with confidence and match-basis metadata
Search canonical SEC entities across issuers, managers, insiders, and funds
Batch resolve shared trace records by trace identifier across filing-derived and supported non-filing datasets
Resolve a single shared trace record by trace identifier across filing-derived and supported non-filing datasets

post.summary: Run a tenant-safe analytical query over supported SEC API history datasets without exposing raw SQL

get.summary: Search filing manifests with historical filters, accession lookup semantics, sorting, and cursor pagination
Retrieve the latest filing for an entity and form
Render the latest filing into Markdown-like text
Extract a section from the latest filing for an entity and form
Return deterministic Item 1A risk-category coverage for the latest covered filing of an issuer
Return the latest board composition derived from definitive proxy filings with director roster and committee coverage semantics
Return the latest SEC N-PORT holdings roster with explicit capability semantics and balance-unit metadata
Retrieve a filing manifest by accession number from the materialized filing corpus
Extract a filing item or section from a specific accession-number filing
Return structured expected pension and retiree benefit payments for a target year when the filing discloses the schedule in rendered tables

Return filing-derived segmented fact history for supported metrics such as revenue and segment profit/loss, with product or geography dimensions, hierarchy metadata, capability state, and trace references when issuers disclose them
Return filing-derived segmented revenue history with XBRL product or geography dimensions, capability state, and trace references when issuers disclose them
Return a share-float wrapper backed by SEC company facts, including disclosed public float when available and shares-outstanding fallback semantics otherwise
Search filing sections and snippets with filing-scoped filters and cursor pagination
Return recent S-1 and 424B prospectus records with cursor pagination and date filters
Return recent Form 144 notices of proposed insider sales with cursor pagination and date filters
Return the list of subsidiaries extracted from the latest 10-K Exhibit 21 for a given entity
Return SEC-native M&A events inferred from public-company filings and relevant exhibits
Return official SEC litigation releases and administrative proceedings with explicit release-source semantics and shared trace references
Return 8-K Item 4.02 restatement and non-reliance events with severity classification and affected-period extraction
Return 8-K Item 4.01 auditor change events with change-type classification (dismissal, resignation, engagement)
Return 8-K Item 5.02 officer and director change events with change-type classification (appointment, departure, resignation, termination)
Return 8-K Item 5.07 voting results with structured proposals, vote counts, and approval outcomes — first-in-market parser ahead of sec-api.io's open bug
Return SEC-furnished earnings materials from 8-K filings with release, remarks, and transcript coverage states
Return market calendar sessions for supported exchanges with source coverage metadata
Return the supported public index roster inventory and optionally include the broader rights-tracked index source catalog
Return rights-safe index constituents for supported benchmark families with canonical entity mapping when available
Return persisted latest-market snapshots for one or more securities with freshness, rights, and revision metadata
Return historical OHLCV bars for a security with materialization and rights metadata
Return rights-reviewed corporate actions used to normalize price history and event analysis
Browse the full ticker universe with pagination, filtering by exchange, type, sector, industry, and search
Return upcoming and recent earnings events with consensus estimates, actuals, and surprise data
Return canonical market reference metadata for a security, including identifiers, exchange, and listing context
Return rights-safe news stories with entity tagging, provenance, and source-rights metadata
Search rights-safe news coverage and issuer communications by symbol, entity, or topic
Search supported macro indicators by keyword across all countries and high-signal packs

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search term matched against indicator key, label, dataset, and series code

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO country code filter (e.g. US, JP, CN)

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.description: Indicator frequency filter

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (default 25, max 100)

get.summary: Return official-source macro indicator observations with revision-aware provenance and country-quality metadata
Return macro release observations with actual, prior, consensus, and surprise metadata
Return the macro event calendar for supported official-source releases and central-bank events
Return SEC API forecast baselines and scenario-aware macro projections with methodology metadata
Return the launch-ring Tier-1 high-signal macro pack with explicit source, fallback, and release-calendar posture for supported countries
Return the current macro regime classification for a country using the canonical SEC API macro overlay
Return sovereign credit ratings from S&P, Moody's, and Fitch for tracked countries and G20 members

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Filter by country name or ISO country code (case-insensitive)

get.summary: Return the sovereign credit rating for a single country by ISO country code

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO 2-letter country code (e.g. US, JP, DE)

get.summary: Return the SEC API factor catalog with methodology, proxy, and orthogonalization metadata

parameters.name: response_mode
parameters.description: Response projection. compact is token-efficient for agents; standard returns the full operational shape; verbose preserves full trust metadata and drill-down context.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated compact-mode expansions such as trust, metadata, series, exposures, position_views, position_exposures, optimizer_candidates, or drilldown. Use trust when an agent or report needs provenance, freshness, source-rights, methodology, revision, and degraded-state metadata.

get.summary: Return a single factor's daily return history with 1D through MAX summary windows for chart and table workflows

parameters.name: factorKey
parameters.description: Canonical factor key or supported public alias such as VALUE_V2. Explicit keys do not broaden to other factors.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Trailing observation window for the returned chart series. Defaults to 1y for token-efficient agent and dashboard workflows. MAX requests the public launch-history floor, 2015-01-01, through the latest complete market date when row-level coverage exists for the factor.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Optional inclusive ISO start date. Must be on or after 2015-01-01. When supplied, it overrides range for the returned series bounds.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Optional inclusive ISO end date. Must be on or after 2015-01-01 and on or before the latest complete XNYS market date.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Return JSON by default or a table-ready CSV export with one row per factor/date.

parameters.name: response_mode
parameters.description: Response projection. compact is token-efficient for agents; standard returns the full operational shape; verbose preserves full trust metadata and drill-down context.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated compact-mode expansions such as trust, metadata, series, exposures, position_views, position_exposures, optimizer_candidates, or drilldown. Use trust when an agent or report needs provenance, freshness, source-rights, methodology, revision, and degraded-state metadata.

expansionHints: Use include=series for full daily observations in compact mode.

get.summary: Return compact multi-factor sparkline series with 1D through MAX summary windows for dashboard, sector, index, and model UIs

parameters.name: factors
parameters.description: Comma-separated canonical factor keys or supported public aliases. Alias normalization is explicit and does not broaden the selection.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Alias for factors.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional launch factor category filter such as style, sector, or industry.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Trailing observation window for the returned sparkline points. Defaults to 1y for token-efficient dashboard and agent workflows. MAX requests the public launch-history floor, 2015-01-01, through the latest complete market date when row-level coverage exists for each factor.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description: Metric used for sparkline point values. Return metrics are cumulative over the selected range; z_score returns point-in-time z-scores.

parameters.name: points
parameters.description: Maximum chart points per factor. The first and last observations are preserved when downsampling.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Optional inclusive ISO start date. Must be on or after 2015-01-01 and overrides range for returned sparkline bounds.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Optional inclusive ISO end date. Must be on or after 2015-01-01 and on or before the latest complete XNYS market date.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Return JSON by default or a table-ready CSV export with one row per factor/date sparkline point.

parameters.name: response_mode
parameters.description: Response projection. compact is token-efficient for agents; standard returns the full operational shape; verbose preserves full trust metadata and drill-down context.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated compact-mode expansions such as trust, metadata, series, exposures, position_views, position_exposures, optimizer_candidates, or drilldown. Use trust when an agent or report needs provenance, freshness, source-rights, methodology, revision, and degraded-state metadata.

get.summary: Return factor moves ranked by unusual z-score or absolute return with direction, threshold, and trust metadata

parameters.name: factors
parameters.description: Comma-separated canonical factor keys or supported public aliases.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Alias for factors.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional launch factor category filter such as style, sector, or industry.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Return window used to score the move.

parameters.name: lookback
parameters.description: History lookback used to compute z-scores. Automatically widens when shorter than window.

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Filter by move direction.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Alias for side, useful for dashboard filters that label move direction directly.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Ranking metric for unusual moves.

parameters.name: min_z_score
parameters.description: Optional absolute z-score floor. Defaults to 0 so the endpoint always returns the top ranked moves.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of ranked factor moves.

parameters.name: response_mode
parameters.description: Response projection. compact is token-efficient for agents; standard returns the full operational shape; verbose preserves full trust metadata and drill-down context.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated compact-mode expansions such as trust, metadata, series, exposures, position_views, position_exposures, optimizer_candidates, or drilldown. Use trust when an agent or report needs provenance, freshness, source-rights, methodology, revision, and degraded-state metadata.

get.summary: Return factor pairs ranked by unusual rolling spread divergence with direction, threshold, and trust metadata

parameters.name: factors
parameters.description: Comma-separated canonical factor keys or supported public aliases used to generate pair combinations.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Alias for factors.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional launch factor category filter such as style, sector, or industry.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Rolling spread window. Calendar aliases map to trading-day pair windows.

parameters.name: lookback
parameters.description: History lookback used to score rolling spread z-scores. Automatically widens when shorter than window.

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Filter by which side of the pair outperformed over the latest window.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Alias for side.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Ranking metric for pair divergence.

parameters.name: min_z_score
parameters.description: Optional absolute z-score floor. Defaults to 0 so the endpoint always returns the top ranked pairs.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of ranked factor pairs.

parameters.name: response_mode
parameters.description: Response projection. compact is token-efficient for agents; standard returns the full operational shape; verbose preserves full trust metadata and drill-down context.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated compact-mode expansions such as trust, metadata, series, exposures, position_views, position_exposures, optimizer_candidates, or drilldown. Use trust when an agent or report needs provenance, freshness, source-rights, methodology, revision, and degraded-state metadata.

get.summary: Return valuation-factor opportunity signals ranked from SecAPI-owned factor return state with trust metadata

parameters.name: factors
parameters.description: Comma-separated valuation factor keys or supported public aliases. Defaults to launch valuation factors.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Alias for factors.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional category filter. Valuation defaults are style factors.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Return window used to score the valuation-factor signal.

parameters.name: lookback
parameters.description: History lookback used to compute factor z-scores. Automatically widens when shorter than window.

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Filter by valuation-factor signal direction.

parameters.name: signal
parameters.description: Alias for side.

parameters.name: weighting_mode
parameters.description: Valuation lens used to transform the factor z-score. The default uses the native long-high-metric / short-low-metric factor; short_leg_focus flips the signal lens for the short leg.

parameters.name: weighting
parameters.description: Alias for weighting_mode.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Ranking metric for valuation-factor opportunities. With side=headwind, opportunity_score ranks the strongest headwinds first while opportunityScore remains signed.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of valuation factor rows.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Return JSON by default or a CSV export for spreadsheet workflows.

parameters.name: response_mode
parameters.description: Response projection. compact is token-efficient for agents; standard returns the full operational shape; verbose preserves full trust metadata and drill-down context.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated compact-mode expansions such as trust, metadata, series, exposures, position_views, position_exposures, optimizer_candidates, or drilldown. Use trust when an agent or report needs provenance, freshness, source-rights, methodology, revision, and degraded-state metadata.

data.opportunitySummary: Value is above trend and inexpensive names have a current factor tailwind.

get.summary: Return stock candidates exposed to a valuation-factor signal using latest materialized factor exposures

parameters.name: factor
parameters.description: Valuation factor key or alias to drill into. If omitted, the top valuation factor is selected.

parameters.name: factorKey
parameters.description: Alias for factor.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Alias for factor.

parameters.name: factors
parameters.description: Comma-separated valuation factor keys used when factor is omitted; the top valuation row is selected.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Alias for factors.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional valuation factor category filter.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Return window used for the factor valuation signal.

parameters.name: lookback
parameters.description: Exposure lookback and valuation z-score lookback.

parameters.name: signal
parameters.description: Optional factor-level valuation signal filter when selecting the top factor.

parameters.name: stance
parameters.description: Stock view: beneficiaries of the factor signal, stocks at risk from it, or both. When omitted, SEC API chooses beneficiaries for tailwind/neutral factors and at_risk for headwind factors.

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Alias for stance; also accepts long, short, all.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Alias for stance.

parameters.name: weighting_mode
parameters.description: Valuation lens used before ranking exposed stocks. Must match the factor-valuation workflow for consistent drilldowns.

parameters.name: weighting
parameters.description: Alias for weighting_mode.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Ranking metric for stock candidates.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of stock candidates.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Return JSON by default or a CSV export for spreadsheet workflows.

parameters.name: response_mode
parameters.description: Response projection. compact is token-efficient for agents; standard returns the full operational shape; verbose preserves full trust metadata and drill-down context.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated compact-mode expansions such as trust, metadata, series, exposures, position_views, position_exposures, optimizer_candidates, or drilldown. Use trust when an agent or report needs provenance, freshness, source-rights, methodology, revision, and degraded-state metadata.

get.summary: Return normalized SEC company facts for an issuer, concept, and optional form or unit. Defaults to the us-gaap taxonomy (10-K/10-Q); when no explicit taxonomy or form is supplied and the us-gaap lookup is empty, automatically falls back to ifrs-full on 20-F/6-K for foreign private issuers (e.g. SAP, TM, SONY, RIO).
Return a normalized statement backed by SEC company facts. Resolves us-gaap (10-K/10-Q) filers and IFRS foreign private issuers (ifrs-full on 20-F/6-K) automatically.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Issuer ticker. Either ticker or cik is required.

parameters.name: cik
parameters.description: Issuer CIK. Either ticker or cik is required.

parameters.name: statement
parameters.description: Statement key such as income_statement, balance_sheet, or cash_flow_statement.

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: Use compact for period-indexed row arrays. Default and agent preserve statement value provenance.

200.description: Successful response. view=compact returns CompactStatement; view=agent returns StatementAgentRecord; default returns Statement.

get.summary: Return combined SEC XBRL-derived income, balance-sheet, and cash-flow statements for a ticker. Covers us-gaap (10-K/10-Q) and IFRS foreign-private-issuer (20-F/6-K) filers automatically.
Return profitability, return, valuation, dividend, and leverage ratios derived from SEC XBRL statements plus market context

Return a multi-axis, revenue-anchored business breakdown (Business Breakdown). Groups SEC XBRL segment facts into the canonical product, geographic, and operating axes, de-subtotals each axis against total reported revenue, and emits per-segment revenue share, profit/loss, margin, and YoY growth. Each axis is flagged reported or low_confidence based on whether it ties to total revenue (banks, REITs, and insurers typically do not tie). Ships beta. Requires ticker or cik.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Issuer ticker. Either ticker or cik is required.

parameters.name: cik
parameters.description: Issuer CIK. Either ticker or cik is required.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Reporting period. Accepts quarter/q aliases. Defaults to annual.

parameters.name: segment_type
parameters.description: Optional restriction to a single canonical axis. Defaults to all canonical axes.

get.summary: Return a first-party SEC company overview — a due-diligence briefing combining identity/classification (name, CIK, tickers, sector/industry from SIC, state of incorporation, exchange, former names) with the latest material filing and a sector-aware financial snapshot derived from multi-year annual SEC XBRL facts. A companyType discriminator (operating / financial_institution / pre_revenue) drives which metrics are valid: financial institutions null gross/operating margin and free cash flow and lean on net margin, ROE, ROA, book value, and total assets. The revenue concept is recency-selected (the candidate whose latest fiscal year is most recent wins) so deprecated tags and wrong sub-lines never win. The base overview is intentionally light; pass include=segments,footnotes,dilution to attach opt-in, bounded enrichments (footnotes is returned as a reference link rather than rendered inline). Ships beta. Requires ticker or cik.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Issuer ticker. Either ticker or cik is required.

parameters.name: cik
parameters.description: Issuer CIK. Either ticker or cik is required.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated opt-in enrichments: segments, footnotes, dilution. Omitted enrichments report status not_requested.

get.summary: List dilution events (S-1, 424B*, FWP, S-3 offerings) with filters by ticker, form, date, and is_atm
Retrieve a single dilution event with verification block, warrants, and convertibles linkage
List warrant schedules parsed from offering exhibits with price-protection and ratchet clauses

List reverse-stock-split actions with execution date and ratio

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Ticker symbol (required). Returns the latest dilution rating for this issuer.

get.summary: Dilution-domain coverage and freshness summary across forms, tickers, and verification status

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Optional ticker scope. When present, counts and freshness are restricted to rows for that issuer.

FreshnessMetadata.description: Freshness of the record. For SEC filings, freshness measures PIPELINE lag — the time between SEC acceptance (`sourcePublishedAt`) and when we materialized the record — NOT the age of the document. Statuses: `current` (recently published and materialized; pipeline keeping up), `archival` (an immutable historical document held in full; its publish→materialize span is not a live-pipeline signal), `degraded`/`stale` (a recently-published filing whose materialization fell behind the freshness thresholds — a genuine ingest problem), `fresh` (continuously-refreshed datasets recently observed at source), `unknown` (no timestamps). An old immutable filing is never `stale` simply because it was published long ago. `lagMs` is the publish→materialize latency in milliseconds.

description.default: Ad hoc model submitted for factor analysis.

FactorCatalogStatus.description: Public launch status for a catalog factor. launch_ready is only emitted when the retained launch metadata proof artifact is ready for the factor.
